title: Canonical Baseline CV=29% — Evaluation Protocol Fundamentally Underpowered

content.finding: Canonical rule training gap has CV=29.2% across seeds (mean=+0.1625, std=0.0475, range +0.0955 to +0.2291). This seed variance drowns out parameter effects. To detect a 7.6% improvement at p<0.05 power=0.8, approximately 116 seeds are needed.
implications: ALL prior 3-5 seed comparisons were underpowered — cannot detect effects smaller than ~30%
Session 6-7 results (5-seed validation) were below statistical detection threshold
The parameter landscape may NOT be flat — effects may exist but be smaller than CV
Future experiments MUST use 50+ seeds minimum, or reduce variance via protocol changes
Alternatively: variance reduction techniques (paired designs, variance-matched seeds, longer runs)
content.key_insight: The evaluation protocol (measure_gap with variable seeds) has inherent CV=29%. This is a methodological constraint, not a substrate limitation. Either increase N dramatically or redesign the measurement to reduce variance.

constraints_implied: Evaluation protocol CV=29% — need 50+ seeds for meaningful comparisons or variance-reduction redesign
